preamble: Elision. The voiceless uvular fricative [χ] is lost, particularly in coda positions. This process reflects a common pattern of weakening and eventual loss of complex consonants in word-final or syllable-final contexts.

examples.note: Dialectal loss of coda uvular fricatives in certain regions.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
